vulnerabilities.title: Conflicting CORS Preflight Whitelisting Headers
vulnerabilities.mechanism: Asynchronous multi-watch background communication channels experience transport terminations (NS_BINDING_ABORTED). The target endpoint drops validation handshakes during cross-origin preflight requests because incoming HTTP OPTIONS tracking headers are missing from the server's Access-Control-Allow-Headers manifest.
vulnerabilities.poc_verification: fetch('https://signaler-pa.clients6.google.com/punctual/multi-watch/channel?VER=8', { method: 'OPTIONS', headers: { 'Origin': 'https://null.zone', 'Access-Control-Request-Method': 'POST', 'Access-Control-Request-Headers': 'cache-control' } }).then(r => console.log('[G-Host CORS] Status:', r.status));

vulnerabilities.title: Information Exposure Through Client-Side Stack Trace Dumps
vulnerabilities.mechanism: The user interface layout surfaces internal framework compilation pathways, active bundle modules (exm), and unmasked parsing errors through the explicit /jserror logging endpoint. When front-end parsing exceptions trigger, internal architecture telemetry leaks directly into outbound parameters.

vulnerabilities.title: Granular Telemetry Exfiltration via Analytics Tag Query Parameters
vulnerabilities.mechanism: The gtag.js ingestion substrate maps browser performance profiles, layout configurations, and localized session hashes (sid) into expansive plain-text parameters. This data array leaks natively across origins within cleartext cross-site GET and XHR POST tracking operations.
